sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports abdominal pain on both sides of the upper abdomen, gradually increasing in severity over the past week. He also reports experiencing intermittent nausea, particularly on and off, and has vomited a couple of times in the past week. The patient reports experiencing nausea. He reports experiencing nausea. The patient reports abdominal pain. The patient reports abdominal pain on both sides of the upper abdomen. The patient reports experiencing nausea.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation in the epigastrium. The patient's heart rate is within normal limits. The patient's blood pressure is mildly low. Lung exam reveals mild end-expiratory wheezes. A grade three out of six systolic ejection murmur is present on heart exam.
medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation in the epigastrium

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are most consistent with acute gastritis, potentially exacerbated by caffeine intake and alcohol consumption.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am prescribing zantac twice daily to alleviate the gastric acid. I am increasing the metformin dosage to 1000 mg in the morning and 500 mg in the evening. I will order a urinalysis, urine pregnancy test, and CBC with CMP to rule out other potential causes. I recommend cutting back on caffeine and spicy foods. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks.

medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation in the epigastrium
